sample.headline: Delhi under intense pre-monsoon heat with 39°C+ temps; Mumbai and Chennai show active monsoon-season conditions with high humidity and significant recent rainfall.
key_drivers: Delhi 30-day avg 36.1°C with only 27mm rain indicates delayed or pre-onset dry heat stress
Mumbai 160mm and Chennai 168mm 30-day precipitation consistent with active southwest monsoon influence
Chennai current low humidity (45%) despite recent rainfall suggests possible break phase or easterly wind shift

risks.note: Delhi current 39.1°C with 35% humidity signals acute heat stress and heatstroke risk for outdoor workers
Mumbai 87% humidity and 160mm recent rain raises urban flooding and waterlogging risk in low-lying areas
Delhi rain deficit (27mm in 30 days) threatens kharif sowing window if monsoon onset continues to lag
Delhi peak demand surge expected as max forecast hits 39.4°C; grid stress and load-shedding risk elevated
Chennai 168mm recent rain improves reservoir inflows but 38.7°C forecast max risks rapid evapotranspiration losses
